judgment.summary: Okta features high gross margins and strong liquidity but faces high leverage and decelerating revenue growth.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last reported EPS of 0.90 beat the 0.85 estimate by 6.3%, with the next earnings date on 2026-05-28.
positives: PEG ratio 0.89 — suggests the stock is undervalued relative to its growth.
Forward P/E 19.55 vs trailing 63.18 — indicates significant expected earnings expansion.
Gross margin 77.4% — reflects high profitability on product sales.
Total cash $2.55B vs total debt $451M — shows a very strong liquidity position.
negatives: Debt/Equity 6.44 — indicates a high level of leverage.
Revenue growth 11.6% — shows relatively modest top-line expansion.
Analyst growth est, current Q -0.8% — suggests a slight expected contraction in growth for the current quarter.

news.paragraph: Okta is navigating a period of volatility following recent security concerns and a significant 23% price jump. While the company is making strategic moves into vendor-neutral AI within a flourishing security sector, recent reports also indicate that shares have been falling. Traders should weigh these strategic AI developments against the backdrop of recent price fluctuations and security-related sentiment.
